t_ch: 拿去卖废品，多少值一点钱。
t_en: I'll scrap it for some pocket money.

t_ch: 这当然是不错，估计能换5个信用点？
t_en: This is pretty good, and you could maybe get about 5 credits for it?

t_ch: 但是如果我告诉你，它远不止这点儿潜力呢。
t_en: But let me tell you: It's far more useful than just that.

t_ch: 这样吧客人，刚才<color=#dbc291ff>「赎珠阁」门前走过了一位青年</color>，一直念叨着「复仇、复仇…」，你去问问他，也许会有些收获。
t_en: How about this, then? Just now, a <color=#dbc291ff>young man just passed by the door of the Jeweler's Pagoda</color> muttering on about "Revenge... Revenge..." Go ask him. It might just be worth your while.

t_ch: 等你有所收获了之后，您再来找我，我再和您讲讲这枚小零件的价值。
t_en: If you manage to get something, come back to me and I'll tell you about the true value of this small component.

t_ch: 关于这个小零件的事儿…
t_en: About this component...

t_ch: 刚才「赎珠阁」门前走过了一位青年，一直念叨着「复仇、复仇…」，你去问问他，也许会有些收获。
t_en: Just now, a young man passed by the door of the Jeweler's Pagoda muttering on about "Revenge... Revenge..." Go ask him. It might just be worth your while.

t_ch: 等你有所收获了之后，您再来找我，我再和您讲讲这枚小零件的价值。
t_en: If you manage to get something, come back to me and I'll tell you about the true value of this small component.

t_ch: 复仇，我必须复仇…
t_en: Revenge. I'll have my revenge...

t_ch: 零件丢到哪儿去了…
t_en: Where did I put that component...

t_ch: 复仇，我必须复仇……
t_en: Revenge, I must get my revenge...

t_ch: 你的戾气好重啊！
t_en: You're giving off a hostile vibe!

t_ch: 戾气重？你说的对，我自己都没有注意……
t_en: Hostile? You're right. I didn't even notice...

t_ch: 你要向谁复仇。
t_en: Who are you taking revenge on?

t_ch: 这与你无关。
t_en: It's nothing to do with you.

t_ch: 冤冤相报何时了。
t_en: If revenge breeds revenge, will there ever be an end to it?

t_ch: 不，我必须以牙还牙，以眼还眼。
t_en: No, I must have a tooth for a tooth, an eye for an eye.

t_ch: 抱歉，我有些太过于不注意他人感受了，我向你道歉。
t_en: I'm sorry. I wasn't paying attention to other people's feelings there. I apologize.

t_ch: 请问您是找我有什么事情吗？
t_en: Is there anything I can do for you?

t_ch: 发生什么事情了？
t_en: What's going on here?

t_ch: 您真是一个善良的人，愿意和我这样的人搭讪。
t_en: You really are a kind person if you're willing to strike up a chat with someone like me.

t_ch: 虽然是个人的私事，告诉您也无妨，我丢了一枚很重要的零件。
t_en: It's a very personal matter, but I guess it's okay to tell you that I've lost a very important component.

t_ch: 那是我父亲工厂里生产的零件…可惜后来无良银行对我们的工厂撤资，最后工厂倒闭了，父亲也受了打击卧床不起。
t_en: It's a component that used to be made in my dad's factory... It's a shame that those heartless bankers pulled their investments from the factory which directly led to its shutdown. My poor dad fell ill then and never really recovered.

t_ch: 我随身带着厂里的小零件，不断提醒自己，要成为一名「善良的银行家」，可惜刚才从星槎上下来的时候，零件弄丢了。
t_en: I like to carry little components from the factory around with me as a reminder to myself to be a "Good Banker." But it's a real shame that I dropped the component when I was getting off the starskiff.

t_ch: 你怎么也是银行工作？
t_en: So you work in a bank, too?

t_ch: 我们家族的仇人就在银行里，要是不靠近他，我要怎么复仇呢？
t_en: Our family's personal rival works in a bank. Keep your friends close, but your enemies even closer...

t_ch: 好像在哪里听说过这个故事…
t_en: I think I've heard this story somewhere before...

t_ch: 您是一位化外民吧，见多识广，肯定听说过不少故事。
t_en: You're an Outworlder, right — you know lots about lots of different things, yeah? You must've heard a lot of stories out there.

t_ch: 哎…要是能找到那枚小零件就好了。
t_en: Sigh... If only I could find dad's missing little component.

t_ch: 你需要这枚小零件吗？
t_en: Do you need this thingamabob?

t_ch: …你怎么知道我弄丢了一枚重要的零件。
t_en: ...How did you know that I lost an important component?

t_ch: 这不是我的那枚零件。
t_en: That's not mine.

t_ch: 我曾经将那枚零件狠狠攥在手里，流出了血…零件的缝隙里留着血渍。而这一枚零件却干干净净，仅是有一些锈渍。
t_en: I remember gripping onto that component so tightly that dug into my skin... There should still be bloodstains on my component. But this one's clean besides the rust stains.

t_ch: 不过，这确实是来自我父亲的零件厂，它依然能够激励我。
t_en: Still, it's from my dad's old factory so it still inspires me.

t_ch: 谢谢你，这枚小小的零件我一定会好好保存的。不过，我不能白白拿走这枚小零件，这样吧，我把这个铁盒送给你吧。
t_en: Thank you. I will hang onto and cherish this. But surely, I can't just take without giving something back, so how about you have this iron box here?

t_ch: 这是什么铁盒？
t_en: What's in the box?

t_ch: 你不会太亏了吗？
t_en: I'm not putting you out too much?

t_ch: 这个铁盒是客户不需要的宝物。说是宝物，你看，它还有个钥匙孔，已经生锈了，钥匙还找不到……
t_en: It's a treasure that a customer no longer needs. By treasure, I mean... Look here, there's a slot for a key that's rusted over. And I don't have the key...

t_ch: 你说它值钱，它也许很值钱，但是我打不开。我们既然有缘，我将这个铁盒给你，希望你能有所收获。
t_en: It may be very valuable indeed, but I can't open it. Since we've been destined to meet, let me gift you this box here in the hope that it's something worthwhile.

t_ch: 谢谢你，找我还有什么事情吗？
t_en: Thank you. Is there anything else I can do?

t_ch: 关于这个铁盒的事儿…
t_en: About this iron box...

t_ch: 客户告诉我，他看到三余书肆里卖铁盒，实在是太感兴趣就买下来了。
t_en: The customer told me that he saw these for sale in the Spare Time Book Shop and couldn't help getting them out of curiosity.

t_ch: 但是他实在是打不开，转手又送给了我。
t_en: But he couldn't get it to open, so he passed it on to me.

t_ch: 我去换掉了小零件…
t_en: I went and replaced the component...

t_ch: 怎么样，我说的没错吧，这枚小零件比你想象得更有价值，还帮你换到了一个铁盒。
t_en: How about it, I was right, wasn't I? This small component was more valuable than you could've possibly imagined — it even managed to get you this iron box.

t_ch: 你是怎么知道那个人需要的？
t_en: How did you know they needed it?

t_ch: 这不过是善于观察而已，雕虫小技，不足挂齿。
t_en: That's just my knack for observational deduction. It's really nothing special.

t_ch: 不过你看，这个铁盒里藏着你不知道的秘密，或许它还有潜在的价值。
t_en: But look here — this box contains mysterious, unknown secrets. Perhaps it's potentially worth something.

t_ch: 现在终于可以卖废品了。
t_en: Now it's finally time to sell some scrap.

t_ch: 如果直接去卖掉，依我估值，这铁盒也就<unbreak>40</unbreak>信用点左右……
t_en: If you sell it directly, you'd only get about <unbreak>40</unbreak> credits based on my initial valuation...

t_ch: 不过你看，这个铁盒里藏着你不知道的秘密，或许它还有潜在的价值。
t_en: But look here — this box contains mysterious, unknown secrets. Perhaps it's potentially worth something.

t_ch: 感觉还可以和谁交换…
t_en: Feels like I can exchange this with someone I know...

t_ch: 看来客人已经深谙此道了。
t_en: Looks like you're already familiar with this hustle, too.

t_ch: 这样吧，我给你一些提示。
t_en: What about this — I'll give you a few hints.

t_ch: 在长乐天有一家叫<color=#dbc291ff>「三余书肆」</color>的书店，去那里看看吧。
t_en: There's a bookstore over in the Exalting Sanctum called the <color=#dbc291ff>Spare Time Book Shop</color>. Go check it out!

t_ch: 请问这里有铁盒吗？
t_en: Excuse me, is there an iron box here?

t_ch: 第二排架子上卖掉了…
t_en: Second rack. Sold out, though...

t_ch: 这里真的没有铁盒吗？
t_en: Are there really no iron boxes here?

t_ch: 以前有本化外民的小说叫《铁盒》，就在右转第二排的架子上，可惜现在已经卖掉了……
t_en: We used to stock a novel by an Outworlder called "Iron Box." It used to be there, second shelf on the right — but it's a real shame, we've already sold out...

t_ch: 我找的不是书，那真的是一个铁盒。
t_en: I'm not looking for a book, but an actual iron box.

t_ch: 可是，我们这里是家书肆，卖的全是书，不卖铁盒。
t_en: But we're a family bookstore here? We sell books, not boxes.

t_ch: 你为什么来书店找铁盒？
t_en: Why did you come looking for a box at a bookstore?

t_ch: 说来都是一段笑谈，您要是不介意的话，我可以全部告诉您。
t_en: It's all a bit of a joke. I'll tell you all about it, if you don't mind.

t_ch: 如你所见，我是一个持明族。我在上一次蜕生之前遇到了一位化外民，他自称是「流光忆庭」的信使，能帮我保存回忆。
t_en: As you can see, I'm a Vidyadhara. Before my last hatching rebirth, I met this Outworlder who claimed to be a messenger from the Garden of Recollection who could help me preserve my memories.

t_ch: 他承诺我，会将我最美的回忆存在「忆泡」之中，而这「忆泡」会小心保存在一个铁盒里，蜕生之后，我便能用那「忆泡」回到过去的记忆里。
t_en: He promised me that he'd store all of my most beautiful memories inside this Memory Bubble which he'd be keeping for me inside this iron box — and that after my hatching rebirth, I could use this Memory Bubble to return to the memories of my past.

t_ch: 前世给我留下笔记和一把钥匙。可惜，书店里没有铁盒，那位使者想必也只是一位骗子…哎。
t_en: My past self left behind a key and a notebook for me. But unfortunately, there're no iron boxes here at the bookstore, so that messenger must have been a scammer... Sigh.

t_ch: 铁盒究竟在哪里呢？
t_en: Where on earth is that iron box?

t_ch: 你需要这个铁盒吗？
t_en: Do you need this iron box?

t_ch: 你刚好有一个铁盒，天下怎么有这么巧的事情？
t_en: You've got an iron box with you? What an incredible coincidence!

t_ch: 也许这都是冥冥注定，铁盒的「忆泡」里有着我最美的回忆……
t_en: This might just be destiny, but this box's Memory Bubble contains all my most precious memories...

t_ch: 这个铁盒不会是和我这把钥匙完全匹配吧？请让我试一试。
t_en: Don't tell me this box matches my key exactly? Let me give it a try.

t_ch: 钥匙虽然与铁盒钥匙孔匹配一致，但钥匙孔内锈蚀化严重，你注意到她尝试了几次终于打开了铁盒——究竟是锁钥匹配，还是强硬捅开，你不得而知。
t_en: The key apparently fits, but the inside of the keyhole is severely rusted. You notice that after several attempts she finally twists open the lid — but you'll never know whether the key fit, or whether it was forced open.

t_ch: …没有「忆泡」，等等，这是照片…戒指…还有一封信。
t_en: There's no Memory Bubble... Hang on, here's a photo... A ring... And a letter to someone.

t_ch: 你等待女子读完了信件，她泪光闪闪，就好像如约收获了最美的回忆。
t_en: You wait as she reads the letter. Tears stream down her face as if her most precious memories are being reaped.

t_ch: 我的前世爱上了一名短生种，却担心他们相爱的经历无人记得，于是编了一个「忆泡」故事，希望我能找到她留下的铁盒，她真是太自私了。
t_en: My past self had fallen in love with this short-life species person, but was afraid nobody would remember their love story. So she made up this caper about a Memory Bubble, hoping I could find the box she left behind. How selfish she was!

t_ch: 为什么会放在书店里呢？
t_en: And why'd you leave it behind at a bookstore?

t_ch: 大概只有我们爱凑热闹的店长才知道了。
t_en: I think only our mischievous friend the shopkeeper knows.

t_ch: 要不是你带来了铁盒，我就错过这些回忆了。这样吧，我也不白拿这铁盒，我把这一罐刚采的「鲛人泪」送给你，也当是我的答谢了。
t_en: If you hadn't brought me the iron box, I'd have missed all of these memories. How about I give you this jar of Draconic Tears to you as a way of saying thanks?

t_ch: 这是什么名贵珍珠吗？
t_en: What priceless treasures are these?

t_ch: 这不就是玻璃珠吗？
t_en: Aren't these just marbles?

t_ch: 眼、眼泪？
t_en: T—Tears?

t_ch: 这是鳞渊境洞天特产的药材，价格不菲，但我这一罐「鲛人泪」品质粗糙，也卖不到什么好价格，怎么处置就交给你了。
t_en: It's a herbal specialty from Scalegorge Waterscape. It's usually quite pricey, but this jar of Draconic Tears here is of lower quality. I won't get much for it, so why don't you take it?

t_ch: 也许，这一罐「鲛人泪」也许还能与人换到什么好宝贝，或者直接回「赎珠阁」，找瀚典掌柜换个好价钱？
t_en: Perhaps you can swap this jar for something special, or try your luck at the Jeweler's Pagoda — I'm sure Pawnbroker Handian will fetch you a good price?

t_ch: 谢谢你，我打算回鳞渊境了。
t_en: Thank you. I'm heading back to Scalegorge Waterscape.

t_ch: 关于这「鲛人泪」的事儿…
t_en: About these Draconic Tears...

t_ch: 我家是做药材生意的，这些「鲛人泪」就是我从鳞渊境里带出来的。
t_en: My family is in the pharmaceutical business, and I've brought these Draconic Tears over from Scalegorge Waterscape.

t_ch: 品质看上去一般，反倒是有些天然去雕饰的意思。听说熟悉仙舟文化的化外民会喜欢这个，但是到哪儿去找这样的化外民呢，你说是吧？
t_en: As average as they may look, they're molded to look like that. I heard discerning Outworlders steeped in the Xianzhou culture like it this way, but how am I ever going to find this kind of Outworlder around?

t_ch: 我换到了一罐「鲛人泪」…
t_en: I swapped it for a jar of Draconic Tears...

t_ch: 没想到客人已经从小零件换到昂贵的药材了。
t_en: I didn't expect you to score expensive pharmaceuticals like that from just a small component.

t_ch: 这罐「鲛人泪」很值钱吧！
t_en: This jar of Draconic Tears is really valuable!

t_ch: 值钱，但是还是不够，我最多给你<unbreak>1000</unbreak>信用点。
t_en: It's valuable, but still not enough. I'll give you <unbreak>1000</unbreak> credits. That's my best offer.

t_ch: 更有价值的做法，客人其实心里明白吧？
t_en: There's another way that's more profitable, and I suspect you know what I mean...

t_ch: 感觉还可以和谁交换…
t_en: Feels like I can exchange this with someone I know...

t_ch: 客人很清楚，宝贝的价值终究是因人而异的。
t_en: You know very well that one man's treasure is another man's trash.

t_ch: 就当这是我最后一次提示了。
t_en: This is my final hint.

t_ch: 听说仙舟上有位从<color=#dbc291ff>星际和平公司远道而来的仙舟通</color>，他可能对「鲛人泪」感兴趣……
t_en: I heard that there's a Xianzhou <color=#dbc291ff>aficionado who came all the way from the IPC</color>. He might be interested in your Draconic Tears...

t_ch: 《厌作人间语》妙啊…
t_en: "Otherworldly Delights," how wonderful...

t_ch: 「鲛人泪」是真的吗？
t_en: Are Draconic Tears real?

t_ch: 你好像对「鲛人泪」感兴趣。
t_en: You seem pretty interested in Draconic Tears.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，看来我自言自语的毛病得改改了。
t_en: Hahaha, I really should correct this bad habit of talking to myself.

t_ch: 最近对仙舟的志怪故事感兴趣得不行，我可以说是「朝思暮想」了。
t_en: I've grown infatuated recently with the mysteries of Xianzhou culture. I've been obsessed with thinking about them...

t_ch: 其中有一篇讲波月古海里，有能活上千年的鲛人，流下眼泪就能化为珍珠，我看了这种志怪故事难免浮想联翩。
t_en: In one of them, there are drakons in the Lunarescent Depths that can live for thousands of years — and their tears crystallize into pearls. It's enough to set the mind racing!

t_ch: 但是那鳞渊境洞天一般不让咱们这样的化外民前去，市场上能买到「鲛人泪」又太过干净了，未免少了些传奇意味。
t_en: But the Scalegorge Waterscape delves generally don't let Outworlders like us go down, and the Draconic Tears out on the market are all too processed, so they lack that legendary authenticity.

t_ch: 你的要求还挺复杂的。
t_en: You sure are fussy.

t_ch: 虽说这是一味药材，但是市面上贩卖药材都看上去都珠光宝气，为了迎合传说故事而清澈得稍显刻意，也许这就是「过犹不及」吧。
t_en: These are pharmaceuticals, but on the open market they always end up looking like sparkly jewels — all scrubbed up nice and pretty to pander to those hunting for legends. Or perhaps that's going a bit too far.

t_ch: 我这人虽然是老外，但是我就喜欢当地人自己的东西，当地人用什么我就用什么。那些干净漂亮的「鲛人泪」都太虚假了，没有收藏价值。
t_en: Me, I may be an Outworlder but I like local customs and use whatever the locals use. Super-clean and clear Draconic Tears are too fake for me, and aren't worth collecting.

t_ch: 我这里有一些不干净的。
t_en: I've got some all-natural ones right here.

t_ch: 就喜欢不干净的「鲛人泪」，让我看看吧。
t_en: I just like natural, unclean Draconic Tears. Let me have a look.

t_ch: 我跟你是一样的。
t_en: Same.

t_ch: 果然我们俩聊得来啊，看来这就是「物以类聚，人以群分」啊，哈哈。
t_en: Looks like we've something in common. Birds of a feather flock together, haha!

t_ch: 既然你和我一样，那你有没有我想要的「鲛人泪」呢？
t_en: Seeing as we're so similar then, have you got any Draconic Tears for me?

t_ch: 其实我这里有一些「鲛人泪」。
t_en: Actually, I've got some Draconic Tears right here.

t_ch: 市场上的「鲛人泪」我见过太多了，希望你能给我看点儿不一样的。
t_en: I've seen too many Draconic Tears on the markets, so I hope you can show me something a little different.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，我第一次看这样玻璃罐子装着的「鲛人泪」…而且，这正是我想要的「有一些粗糙的鲛人泪」，谢谢你啦。
t_en: Hahaha, this is the first time I've seen Draconic Tears inside a glass jar like this... And this is exactly the kind of authentic Draconic Tears that I'm looking for! Thank you so much.

t_ch: 喂，你不讲规矩啊。
t_en: Hey, you gotta play by the rules.

t_ch: 什么规矩？
t_en: What rules?

t_ch: 以物换物的规矩。
t_en: The rules of barter.

t_ch: 别让我说出来嘛。
t_en: Don't make me say it.

t_ch: 请你礼尚往来。
t_en: Please could you reciprocate in kind?

t_ch: 明白明白，往而不来，非礼也；来而不往，亦非礼也。
t_en: Ah, got it. "Do unto others as you would have them do unto you."

t_ch: 我身上也没有带什么值钱的东西，这个小礼物就送给你了，不成敬意。
t_en: I didn't bring anything valuable with me today, so here's something very small for you as thanks.

t_ch: 集装箱门打开，你看到里面站着两个怪家伙。看得出来，这是两个药王秘传的莳者在秘密会谈。
t_en: The container doors open and you see two weird people standing inside. You can tell that this is a secret meeting between some disciples of Sanctus Medicus.

t_ch: 你不认识他们，于是依着仙舟戏曲的习俗，在心中给他们起了「匪兵甲」和「匪兵乙」的名号。
t_en: You don't know them, so based on the conventions of Xianzhou Opera you decide to name them "Bandit A" and "Bandit B."

t_ch: 你们面面相觑，六目相对，气氛颇为尴尬。这时，你问出了那个此时最该问的问题——
t_en: The three of you all look at each other in blank dismay and the vibe starts to feel a little awkward. Right then, you decide to ask the most appropriate question of all at that time:

t_ch: 你们在干嘛？
t_en: What are you guys doing?

t_ch: 你们是谁？
t_en: Who are you guys?

t_ch: 聊啥呢？
t_en: What are we talking about?

t_ch: 你好？
t_en: Hello?

t_ch: …你是？
t_en: ...And you are?

t_ch: 药王慈怀，建木生发。
t_en: Mercy of Sanctus Medicus, the Arbor's new growth.

t_ch: 莳者一心，同登仙道！原来是自己人啊，吓我一跳，还以为是神策府那帮猎狗找来了呢。
t_en: Unity of Discipuli Medici, the Immortality's true path! So you're one of us then. You really freaked me out — I thought you were one of those stupid dogs from the Seat of Divine Foresight.

t_ch: 恰巧路过的英雄。
t_en: Just a hero who happens to be passing by.

t_ch: …呃呃，什么玩意儿？
t_en: ...Errr, what the heck?

t_ch: …你是？
t_en: ...And you are?

t_ch: 我是…我是谁关你什么事啊？
t_en: I am... What does it matter to you who I am?

t_ch: 等会儿，这人是不是有点眼熟？
t_en: Hang on. Doesn't this guy seem familiar to you?

t_ch: *仙舟粗口*！我想起来了！这家伙是景元的客人，是罗浮外面来的猎狗！
t_en: *Xianzhou profanity*! I remember now! This is Jing Yuan's guest, an Outworlder dog!

t_ch: 既然被你认出，只好动手了！
t_en: You got me. Guess we'd better fight!

t_ch: 好骨气！来吧！
t_en: That's the spirit! Bring it on!

t_ch: 拜拜，我先走了。
t_en: Bye for now, I'm off.

t_ch: 快上，这*仙舟粗口*想跑！
t_en: Quick, this *Xianzhou profanity* wants to run!

t_ch: 哇，终于重见天日了！差点以为要直接给老子打包发送到法珊泽尔了！
t_en: Finally, daylight! I thought I was going to be sent off to Farschanzel!

t_ch: 你为什么在里面？
t_en: What were you doing in there?

t_ch: 我被人骗进了这个陷阱啊！你敢信吗？这世上竟有人坏到去欺骗一个单纯的阴晴伞！
t_en: I got tricked into this trap! Can you believe it? I never thought there'd be people in this world who could cheat pure and simple Baroumbrella!

t_ch: 法珊泽尔是哪里？
t_en: Where's Farschanzel?

t_ch: 认真的吗？你打开一个集装箱，里面钻出来一个会说话的阴晴伞，而你此时最想问的问题竟然是「法珊泽尔是哪里？」
t_en: Are you kidding me? You've just opened up a container and found a talking mushroom inside, and the first thing you want to ask me is "Where's Farschanzel"?

t_ch: 这大概说明你是真的见多识广……
t_en: This probably demonstrates that you're well-traveled and experienced...

t_ch: 总之，很感谢你把我救出来。
t_en: Anyway, thank you very much for helping me out of here.

t_ch: 刚才我正在开开心心在路上飞，突然冒出一个小屁孩，跟我说：「我这里有小阴晴伞不能看的好东西！」
t_en: Just now I was flying along the road, happy as can be when this kid appeared out of nowhere and told me: "I've got something cool here that little Baroumbrella can't see!"

t_ch: 我一听就来精神了啊！毫不犹豫地就跟着他走了，然后他就骗我钻进了这个集装箱……
t_en: I got really fired up when I heard that! So I followed him without hesitation, and before long he'd tricked me into this container here...

t_ch: 这个集装箱里货物特别多啊，我还以为都是…都是…都是「小阴晴伞不能看的好东西」……
t_en: There's so much packed into this container, and I thought it was... I thought it was... "Cool stuff that little Baroumbrella can't see"...

t_ch: 结果仔细一看才发现，他所谓「小阴晴伞不能看的好东西」，竟然是几大箱发往域外的蘑菇干！
t_en: But on closer inspection, I realized that all the "cool stuff that little Baroumbrella can't see" was actually just a container stuffed full of dried mushrooms — and addressed to an off-world destination!

t_ch: 我刚想怒斥这孩子，就看到他狞笑着锁上了集装箱的门…要不是你来得及时啊，我现在真的就被寄到法珊泽尔了！和那些蘑菇干一起！
t_en: Just as I was about to scold this child, I saw him grinning as he locked the gate to the container... If you hadn't arrived when you did, I'd be halfway to Farschanzel by now! Just like all those dried mushrooms!

t_ch: 总之，这里还有些好东西，就当做是我的谢礼吧。
t_en: Anyway, there's still some good stuff here. Take it as a gift for rescuing me.

t_ch: 我先撤了，拜拜。
t_en: I'm outta here. See ya.

t_ch: 集装箱的门打开时，两人显然吓了一跳，直勾勾地看着你。看起来，这是一对正在共度美好时光的情侣。
t_en: As the door to the container opens, two people stare at you, petrified. Looks like you've just walked in on a couple of lovers trying to have a great time with each other.

t_ch: 你不认识他们，于是依着仙舟戏曲的习俗，在心中给他们起了「小梁」和「小祝」的名号。
t_en: You don't know them, so based on the conventions of Xianzhou Opera you decide to name them "Rom" and "Com."

t_ch: 你你你你你你是谁？为什么突然开门？
t_en: Wh—Wh—Wh—Who are you? What's all the rush?

t_ch: 不好意思…
t_en: Excuse me...

t_ch: 我只是路过的寻宝者…
t_en: I'm just a treasure hunter passing through...

t_ch: 打扰了，你们继续…
t_en: Don't mind me, as you were...

t_ch: 吓死我了，我还以为是家里人来抓我们了。
t_en: You freaked us out! I thought you were my family coming to bust us.

t_ch: 家里人？
t_en: Your family?

t_ch: 我们两个的家族从三劫时代开始就不怎么融洽，可偏偏到了我们这一代，竟在学宫中义无反顾地相爱了。
t_en: Ever since the Three Sufferings Era, our families have been at each other's throats — but this generation, we fell head over heels in love with each other at the academy.

t_ch: 家人为了拆散我们，想了很多办法…但爱又是如何能被外力所消解的呢？所以，我们决定藏在这艘「化蝶号」货运星槎上，偷偷离开罗浮……
t_en: Our families keep trying to split us up, all these different schemes... But how can true love be broken from the outside? So that's why we hid out here on this freight starskiff, the SS Butterfly: We want to sneak off the Luofu...

t_ch: 陌生人，在此相逢也算是有缘…收下这些吧，这些财物是我们与原生家庭最后的联结，将它们交给你，我们二人便与那两家人再无关系了！
t_en: Well, stranger, I guess it's fate that we met each other here, too... Take these. They're the last remaining ties we have to our families. If you take these, then the two of us will have nothing more to do with either of our families!

t_ch: 娘子说得对…收下吧，就当是对我们这段缘分的见证。
t_en: She's right... Take these, as a testimony to our destiny.

t_ch: 再见了，陌生人。愿你与所爱之人可以平安幸福，直到宇宙的终结。
t_en: So long, stranger. May you and your loved ones remain safe and happy until the universe ends.

t_ch: 不等你做出回答，小梁和小祝就乘着「化蝶号」一起离开了码头。
t_en: Without even waiting for an answer, Rom and Com jump aboard the SS Butterfly, and depart the jetty.

t_ch: 呃…你怎么解开的这么快？
t_en: Ugh... How'd you solve it so quickly?

t_ch: 怎么了？
t_en: Something the matter?

t_ch: 说来惭愧，我还没来得及准备宝箱…因为准备放在宝箱里的礼物迟迟没有到货。
t_en: I'm ashamed to say that I haven't had time to prepare a chest... Because the gift I was going to put inside hasn't been delivered yet.

t_ch: 唉…让你白跑一趟，真对不起。这样吧，给你一个取货码。
t_en: Ugh... Sorry about wasting your time, I'm really sorry. How about this, I'll give you a collection code for the item?

t_ch: 你去星槎海找梓桥，告诉她这个取货码，就能拿到我原本准备好的「宝箱」了。
t_en: Go and see Ziqiao in Starskiff Haven and tell her this code. She'll be able to give you the chest that I was going to give you.

t_ch: 那我先走了，祝你一切顺利！再见！
t_en: Alright, then I'm heading out. Wishing you all the best! So long!

t_ch: 你还没反应过来，她就风风火火地离开了码头。
t_en: Before you could react, she shot off from the jetty.

t_ch: 我要取个货。
t_en: I'm picking something up.

t_ch: 要取货？好啊，取货码拿来我看一下。
t_en: Alright, give me the collection code please.

t_ch: 我看看…没问题，你的货是这件。不过，你的这件货物是货到付款的。价格…需要<unbreak>1000</unbreak>信用点。
t_en: Let me see... Not a problem, here you go. But you do know this item is cash-on-delivery, right? It's... <unbreak>1000</unbreak> credits, please.

t_ch: 货到付款？
t_en: Cash-on-delivery?

t_ch: 搞错了吧？
t_en: Could there be a mistake?

t_ch: 这可是「宝箱」啊！
t_en: C'mon, it's a chest!

t_ch: 那我确认一下哦…确实没搞错。你得付款，我才能把货物给你。当然了，你也可以拒收，我们会把货物退给寄件人。
t_en: Okay, I'll check that again for you... And it's no mistake. Please pay first, and then I'll be able to release the item to you. Of course, you're welcome to decline, and I can just return it to the sender.

t_ch: 好，我交钱。
t_en: Alright, here's the money.

t_ch: 好的。货款已确认收到，请拿好你的货物～
t_en: Okay, payment received. Please take your item~

t_ch: 你打开邮包，看到里面静静地躺着一张星际和平公司的储值卡，面值<unbreak>1000</unbreak>信用点。
t_en: You open up the parcel and see a stored-value card from the IPC just lying there, with a face value of <unbreak>1000</unbreak> credits.

t_ch: 我拒收好了。
t_en: I refuse.

t_ch: 好的。不过退货会产生邮费，需要支付<unbreak>1000</unbreak>信用点。
t_en: Alright, but in that case there's a return fee. It's <unbreak>1000</unbreak> credits.

t_ch: 凭什么啊？
t_en: And why?

t_ch: 我不能也选到付吗？
t_en: Can't that be cash-on-return, too?

t_ch: 因为这是到付邮件，所以拒收退件是不能选择到付的…不然如果对方选择赖账，我们就要承担损失了。
t_en: This parcel is cash-on-delivery, so returns can't be cash-on-return, I'm afraid... Otherwise, we'd be on the hook for any losses if the debt's reneged on.

t_ch: 这是我们的规章制度，不好意思啊。
t_en: I'm sorry, but that's in our rules and regulations.

t_ch: 我再想想…
t_en: Let me think...

t_ch: 没事，你慢慢考虑。
t_en: No problem. Take your time.

t_ch: 我再想想…
t_en: Let me think...

t_ch: 没事，你慢慢考虑。
t_en: No problem. Take your time.

t_ch: 先别开门！等一下！我现在没穿衣服！
t_en: Don't come in! Wait up! I'm naked!

t_ch: 我报警了。
t_en: I'm calling the cops.

t_ch: 别！我不是什么坏人，我这是有苦衷的！
t_en: No! I'm not a bad guy. I've got a lot going on!

t_ch: 出什么事了？
t_en: What's happened?

t_ch: 虽然说来你未必会信，但我是有苦衷的！
t_en: You might not believe me but... I've got a lot going on!

t_ch: 我是学宫的算学教授…可能我把所有精力都分配给算学了吧，所以有时候…在日常生活中会比较粗心……
t_en: I'm a math professor at the academy... Perhaps it's because I throw myself into my math research so much, sometimes... I can get careless in my everyday life.

t_ch: 尤其是灵感到来的时候，我更是完全顾不上现实世界中那些远不如数学法则简洁优美的东西……
t_en: Especially when I get that hot shot of inspiration, I couldn't give a hoot about any of that real-world stuff which is far less simple and majestic than my beautiful math...

t_ch: 今天也是，我正在研究能否用宇宙际玛姬诺夫理论来论证费尔盖=邵宇猜想，一边思索着就一边出门散步了……
t_en: Today's no difference. Right now I'm looking into whether or not the interstellar Maginov theory can demonstrate the Felgay-Shaoyu hypothesis, so I've decided to take a walk and ponder things...

t_ch: 直到听到路上有人尖叫，我才意识到，自己竟然忘记穿衣服了！我一路逃窜，躲到了这个货船里……
t_en: It wasn't until I heard a piercing scream that I realized I'd forgotten to put on my clothes! I scurried away in a flash and ended up hiding here in this freight skiff...

t_ch: 路过的好心人，能不能请你帮我取一下衣服…去星槎海找「广云袖」的予衿小姐，和她说是「那位老师」的请托，她就能明白！
t_en: O, generous stranger, could you please help me get some clothes to wear... If you could find a Miss Yujin at Cloudbreadth Sleeves over in the Starskiff Haven and tell her "that teacher" needs help, she'll know what I mean!

t_ch: 求求你了！救救我吧！我一定会报答你的大恩大德！
t_en: I'm begging you! Help me, please! I'd love to repay your extraordinary kindness!

t_ch: （嗯？怪了，舱门怎么没打开？是不是哪里卡住了？）
t_en: (Huh? That's weird. The hatch won't open? Is it stuck somewhere?)

t_ch: 我来帮「那位老师」取东西。
t_en: I'll help "that teacher" with his fetch errand.

t_ch: 啊？「那位老师」啊…又来了…好的，我知道了，请您稍等。
t_en: Mmm? "That teacher"... Again... Okay, I know, I know. Give me a minute.

t_ch: 这个包裹里就是「那位老师」需要的东西。他已经付过钱了，您直接拿给他就好。
t_en: This parcel here, it's got everything "that teacher" needs. He's already paid for it, so you can just take it over for him.

t_ch: （前一艘货运星槎还没离港…大概因为「那位老师」还被困在里面。去找予衿帮他要件衣服吧。）
t_en: (The last freight starskiff hasn't left the port yet... Probably because "that teacher" is still stuck inside. Go find Yujin and help get his clothes.)

t_ch: 太好了，你回来了！我刚才差一点点就以为你不会回来了！快，顺着快递箱的门缝把衣服递给我吧，我开始觉得冷了……
t_en: Excellent, you're back! I almost thought you weren't going to come back for me! Quickly, pass me the clothes through the courier package. It's starting to feel a little chilly...

t_ch: 真是太感谢你的大恩大德了！集装箱里这些东西，是我全身上下最值钱的东西了，请你收下吧。
t_en: Thank you so much for your inestimable kindness! These things in the container here are the most valuable possessions I own. You've earned them.

t_ch: 你把宝箱藏哪儿了？
t_en: Where'd you hide the chest?

t_ch: 我寻思你也没穿衣服啊？
t_en: I'm guessing you're naked again?

t_ch: 你误会了，这整艘货运星槎都是我的。当时就算我再慌不择路，也不能闯进别人的货运星槎里吧？我可是很有涵养的！
t_en: You're mistaken. This entire freight starskiff is all mine. Even given how panicked I was, there's still no way I could sneak into someone else's freight starskiff, right? I'm a conservative guy!

t_ch: 那我先走了啊…刚闹出了这么丢脸的事，我实在没脸面在市面上晃了……
t_en: Well, I'll be going then... Such an embarrassment for me, there's no way I can show my face at the markets again...

t_ch: 我一眼就认出你来了。
t_en: I recognized you straight away.

t_ch: 你好，我是「枘凿六合」秘密结社的「智囊」。说是「智囊」，这也只是我的代号而已。
t_en: Hello there, I'm Think-Tank from the secret society Hexanexus. Think-Tank is just my code name in the club.

t_ch: 毕竟魔方秘社只是一个隐秘的组织，即便是在工造司内，也鲜少有人知道我们的成员结构，成员之间都是用代号称呼彼此的。
t_en: After all, it's just a secret society, and not even the rest of our Artisanship Commission colleagues know who our members are. We all call each other by our code names.

t_ch: 抱歉，光顾着自己说话了，你肯定有很多疑惑，你可以随便问我。
t_en: Sorry, I've been waffling on. You must have a lot of questions, so ask me whatever you'd like.

t_ch: 你们竟然真的是秘密组织？
t_en: Are you really a secret society?

t_ch: 没错，工造司原本就有着一群热爱「枘凿六合」的匠人，但是我们的爱好常常不被世人所理解，总是有人对我们抱有不解和敌意。
t_en: That's right. There used to be a group of us craftsmen in the Artisanship Commission who love the Hexanexus. But normies never really got us, so there's always someone trying to hate on us.

t_ch: 后来我们选择成立秘密组织，为的就是办好一件事情——
t_en: Later on, we set up this secret society in order to do one thing, and do it well—

t_ch: 让全世界都充满「枘凿六合」！让人们沉浸在「枘凿六合」的幸福里。
t_en: To saturate the world with the Hexanexus! To let everyone immerse themselves in the pleasure of the Hexanexus.

t_ch: 你们都是怎样招募新成员的？
t_en: How do you guys find new recruits?

t_ch: 主要分为两种形式，一种为「社会招收」，一种为「内部推荐」。
t_en: There're two main ways actually: One is called "social recruitment," and the other way is by "internal recommendation."

t_ch: 那些在路边偶然玩到了「枘凿六合」，爱上了「枘凿六合」的，我们称之为「社会招收」，对象广泛但效率不高。
t_en: We call it social recruitment when there's someone on the roadside obsessing over the Hexanexus. This casts a wide net, but few candidates are successful.

t_ch: 成员之间相互引荐，由信赖的成员担保新成员加入的，我们称之为「内部推荐」，招收对象值得信赖。
t_en: Members also make recommendations among themselves, and if trustworthy members vouch for a new recruit, we call this recruitment an "internal recommendation." These candidates are trustworthy.

t_ch: 无论是哪种加入方式，加入者都必须热爱「枘凿六合」。
t_en: No matter which way you come to us, everyone MUST be passionate about the Hexanexus.

t_ch: 你们秘社里还有些什么代号？
t_en: What other code names have you got in your secret society?

t_ch: 你对我们的代号感兴趣啊……
t_en: So you wanna know about our code names, huh...

t_ch: 大家取代号都挺随意的，有自己取的，比如「说书人」、「丹青师」、「机巧士」……
t_en: Everyone's code name is pretty random. Some pick names themselves, like Storyteller, Danqing Artist, the Ingeniator...

t_ch: 也有人直接沿用别人用过的代号，比如我这「智囊」，很多人都当过「智囊」。
t_en: And some just adopt a nickname that's been picked out by someone else, like me — Think-Tank. Lots of people have been "Think-Tanks" before.

t_ch: 为什么选在这么偏僻的地方？
t_en: Why did you choose such an out-of-the-way place like this?

t_ch: 我们毕竟是个秘密组织嘛。
t_en: Well, we're a secret society, after all.

t_ch: 再说了，这附近就有「枘凿六合」，我们一会儿交流起来更加方便。
t_en: Besides, there's the Hexanexus nearby, so it's easier for us to link up over here.

t_ch: 开始聊正事吧。
t_en: Let's get down to business then.

t_ch: 很好，闲聊就到此为止，首先，我要授予你「秘社终身社员」的称号，恭喜你。
t_en: Great, that's enough chit-chat. First off, I'd like to confer the title of Lifetime Hex Club Membership onto you. Congratulations.

t_ch: 接下来想问你的是，在你看来，我们要怎样才能「吸量」，我们要怎样才能提高「次日留存」，我们要怎样才能成为「爆款」？
t_en: Next, I'd like to ask you — in your opinion — how can we attract more recruits? How can we improve our next-day retention? How can we go viral?

t_ch: 「枘凿六合」不是挺好的吗？
t_en: Isn't the Hexanexus a marvelous invention?

t_ch: 可是你也看到了，这么久以来，根本没有多少人愿意关注「枘凿六合」。所以，秘社才会想办法骗更多的人来玩。
t_en: However, as you can see, this whole time we've struggled to get anyone to play the Hexanexus. So this whole "secret society" is a way of tricking more people into playing it.

t_ch: 这就是你们「创造一切的初心」吗？
t_en: Is that what you mean by "Aspiring to Create Everything"?

t_ch: 那只是口号罢了。
t_en: Well, that's just a slogan.

t_ch: 抱歉，没想到你是个这么感性的人，我问错问题了。
t_en: Sorry, I didn't expect you to be such a perceptive person. I asked the wrong question.

t_ch: 你不应该问我，而是问分析师。
t_en: Don't ask me. Ask an analyst.

t_ch: …你说的有道理，这不应该询问你。
t_en: ...You have a point. I shouldn't be asking you about this.

t_ch: 你问的这些东西我都不知道。
t_en: I don't have any answers to these questions of yours.

t_ch: 看来，是我有些急于求成了。
t_en: Seems like I'm a little impatient for results here.

t_ch: 这些问题一直困扰着我们，但我好像问错人了。
t_en: These issues have been plaguing us forever, but I think I'm seeking answers from the wrong guy.

t_ch: 「枘凿六合」玩法不行。
t_en: The Hexanexus gameplay is no good.

t_ch: …我很难认同你的观点。
t_en: ...I have a hard time agreeing with your point of view.

t_ch: 那么，我换个问题吧，我们应该如何让「枘凿六合」变得更好玩一些呢？
t_en: Then let me change the question. How can we make the Hexanexus even more fun?

t_ch: 还应该增加其他维度。
t_en: Add in extra dimensions.

t_ch: 其他维度？现在已经有「虚」和「实」了，除此之外…还能再增加花纹。
t_en: Extra dimensions? We already have Virtual and Real. And... We could also put in some more decorative patterns.

t_ch: 现在的难度还是太低了。
t_en: It's not difficult enough.

t_ch: 难度太低？要是在方块上增加「花纹对应」的要求，也许还能更难。
t_en: It's too easy!? If we added a pattern-matching element to the game, it should be even more difficult.

t_ch: 装置的造型太单调了。
t_en: The device shape is too plain.

t_ch: 造型单调？要是在方块上增加「花纹」，说不定就能打破这种单调感……
t_en: The shape is too plain? If we add in some decorative patterning, that would maybe shake up things...

t_ch: 这样吧，你稍等一下，我去给「枘凿六合」加上「花纹」。
t_en: Okay, wait a second. I'm going to add some decorative patterns to this Hexanexus.

t_ch: 「智囊」不愧是工造司出身的工匠，「枘凿六合」经过他的改造，已经改头换面。
t_en: No wonder Think-Tank is such an accomplished craftsman at the Artisanship Commission. The Hexanexus is completely modified as per his specifications.

t_ch: 你现在去试试看吧。
t_en: Why don't you give it a try now?

t_ch: 怎么样，怎么样，「枘凿六合」变得更好玩了吗？
t_en: How about it, then? Is the Hexanexus even more fun now?

t_ch: 确实变好玩了。
t_en: It really is more fun now.

t_ch: 那可真是太好了啊，新版本的「枘凿六合」肯定大受欢迎。
t_en: That's great. This new Hexanexus model is gonna be a hit.

t_ch: 不太清楚。
t_en: Not too sure.

t_ch: 不清楚的话，多试几次肯定就明白了。
t_en: Give it a few more tries and you'll definitely come around to it.

t_ch: 反而变得更不好玩了。
t_en: Just the opposite — it's less fun now.

t_ch: 我不信，你让我再多去做几个你试试。
t_en: I don't believe it. Let me make a few more custom changes for you.

t_ch: 不愧是「枘凿六合」的深度测试用户，你的建议简直是醍醐灌顶。
t_en: You really are a Hexanexus power tester! These suggestions of yours are simply illuminating.

t_ch: 魔方秘社很快就会将所有的「枘凿六合」升级改造，位置就在你之前玩过的地方。
t_en: We'll soon have all of our Hexanexus games upgraded. You can find one where you were playing it before.

t_ch: 这可是个大版本更新啊，干脆就叫「枘凿六合<unbreak>2.0</unbreak>」吧。
t_en: This is a major update, so we may as well call this the Hexanexus <unbreak>2.0</unbreak>.

t_ch: 真的有人会去玩吗？
t_en: Will anyone actually play it?

t_ch: 新版本肯定会吸引新人的。
t_en: This new version is sure to be a hit.

t_ch: 再说了，我们希望你也要继续玩啊。
t_en: Besides, we hope that you can keep playing as well.

t_ch: 不发布个前瞻预告吗？
t_en: Aren't you going to post a preview?

t_ch: 我会考虑一下的，如果这样做有宣传效果的话。
t_en: I'll think about it when we come to promotions.

t_ch: 除此之外，这次新的「枘凿六合」里面，我们也会放置宝箱，你放心，只要你玩「枘凿六合」，我们就是异父异母的好{F#姐妹}{M#兄弟}，这些宝箱都归你。
t_en: Oh yeah, we'll place chests inside our new Hexanexus this time. Don't worry about it. Just play the Hexanexus and you'll get your chests — now that you're my good {F#Sister}{M#Brother}.

t_ch: 再见了…啊，差点儿忘了，给。这是我承诺给你的「秘社终身社员」奖励。
t_en: Goodbye then... Ah, I almost forget: Here you go. This is your gift as our latest Lifetime Hex Club Membership.

t_ch: 请一定要多玩啊，「枘凿六合」天下第一！
t_en: Play lots and play often. Hexanexus is the bestest!

t_ch: 你看看你看看，这地方都是些什么人啊。
t_en: Just look! Look at the kinds of people that come here.

t_ch: 有逃避现实醉生梦死的，有满嘴谎言虚情假意的，还有又哭又闹求安慰的…也对，哪个靠谱的人会到梦里来？
t_en: There are daydreamers escaping from reality, liars and hypocrites, and tantrums from squabblers desperate for comfort... That's right. What kind of dependable person would ever venture into a dream?

t_ch: 依我看，匹诺康尼就是个大垃圾桶，装满了来自全宇宙的失败者——遇到一点小挫折就狂刷弱保软。
t_en: By my reckoning, Penacony is a huge trashcan filled with losers from all over the universe — who turn into babies the second they come across the slightest setback.

t_ch: 在这种地方待久了，你会不自觉地堕落下去——你懂吗？你的思维、情感，都会在纵欲中被磨钝，到最后整个人就废了！
t_en: Spend too long in a place like this, and you'll start to degenerate without realizing... Do you understand? Your thoughts, your emotions — everything is blunted by over-indulgence and in the end you'll become useless!

t_ch: 那你觉得该怎么办呢？
t_en: So what should we do, then?

t_ch: 你说的都对…
t_en: Everything you said was true...

t_ch: 不要以为我看不出你暗戳戳的讽刺。你已经打心眼里开始认同这种生活方式了，是不是？
t_en: Don't think I can't tell that's biting sarcasm. You've already started to endorse this kind of lifestyle from the bottom of your heart, haven't you?

t_ch: （沉默不语）
t_en: (Remains silent)

t_ch: 娱乐就是对宇宙资源的浪费、对普通人努力的亵渎！匹诺康尼这种地方，早该关停了！
t_en: Entertainment is a grand waste of the universe's resources, and disrespectful to the hard work of everyday people! Places like Penacony should've been shut down a long time ago!

t_ch: 试想一下吧，这片宇宙孕育了多少文明种族，其中有能力离开母星的占多少？真正迈出那关键一步，选择踏上星际旅途的又有多少？
t_en: Think about it: How many civilizations has this universe spawned? And how many have star-faring capabilities? How many actually make the pivotal choice to set out on an intergalactic journey?

t_ch: 有机会走出家乡、探索星空的人，要么是亿中挑一的幸运儿，要么付出了常人难以想象的代价——我就是后者之一。
t_en: Anyone with the chance to leave their homeworld behind and explore the universe has either one-in-a-billion luck, or has paid a price unfathomable to ordinary people. I am one of the latter.

t_ch: 为了离开母星，我寒窗苦读数十载，终于敲开了博识学会的大门——再看看这里的人，他们把这来之不易的机会用来干什么了？花天酒地！
t_en: I studied painstakingly for decades to leave my home system before finally entering the hallowed realm of the Intelligentsia Guild — and just look at the people here. What have they done with this hard-earned opportunity of theirs? Live out a life of debauchery!

t_ch: 母星费尽心思养育你，是希望你能够建设它，不是为了让你放肆享乐！
t_en: Your home planet puts all these efforts into raising you out of hope that you can build it up — not for your wanton pleasure!

t_ch: …这么简单的道理，这些人怎么就不明白呢？！
t_en: ...How can these people not understand such simple truths!?

t_ch: 我就不一样了，我没有母星。
t_en: I'm different. I don't have a home planet.

t_ch: 男人先是沉默，随后用复杂的眼神看向你，很难判断那眼神中传达的是怜悯还是惊奇。
t_en: The man falls silent before shooting you a complicated look with his eyes. It is impossible to tell whether it is a look of pity, or wonder.

t_ch: 既然如此…那咱们也别再计较了。去做你想做的事情吧。
t_en: Notwithstanding that being the case... Let's stop bickering about it. Go, do whatever you want to do.

t_ch: 说罢，他又换上那副脸谱般的愤懑表情，重新投入到对路人的批判中。
t_en: He placed his indignant expression back on his face before throwing himself back into his people-watching and criticism.

t_ch: 你的生活想必很不如意吧。
t_en: Your life must be very unhappy indeed.

t_ch: 你，我…你居然人身攻击！
t_en: You, I... that's a personal attack!

t_ch: 他的头发和眉毛因愤怒拧成一团，好一会儿之后才舒展开来。
t_en: His hair and brow knitted together in a rage, taking quite some time before they unfurl.

t_ch: …哼，我才不跟你一般见识！我好歹也是博识学会的成员，必须保持涵养……
t_en: ...Hmm, I'm not stooping to your level! I'm a member of the Intelligentsia Guild and ought to maintain self-discipline...

t_ch: 年轻人，回头是岸——这是我唯一能对你说的了。
t_en: Youngster, there's always a pathway back... That's all I can say to you.

t_ch: 啊对对对。
t_en: Ah yes, hmm, I see.

t_ch: 男人陷入了长久的沉默，只能用眼神向你投以回答。
t_en: The man sank into a long silence and could only reply to you with a look.

t_ch: 那眼神仿佛在说：你怕不是把脑子玩坏了。
t_en: That look seemed to be saying: I'm afraid you've lost your mind.

t_ch: 哎，整天这样醉生梦死，能取得成功就怪了！
t_en: Ah, it'd be a miracle to see any success through fuddling about in a drunken stupor like this all day.

t_ch: 很多来宾已经成功了。
t_en: Lots of guests have already succeeded.

t_ch: 啊，这个…呃……
t_en: Ah, about that... Er...

t_ch: 如、如果没有玩物丧志的话，他们本可以变得更成功，取得更大的成就——每在玩乐上浪费一秒，你能给这个宇宙做贡献的时间就减少了一分。
t_en: I—If they weren't obsessed with minutiae, then they could be even greater successes making even greater achievements — every second wasted on leisure is another minute lost that you could have devoted to saving the universe.

t_ch: 只能说懂的人都懂，不懂的…那是朽木不可雕也！
t_en: All I can say is, those who get it REALLY get it, and those who don't... Well, it's no use carving rotten wood, is it?

t_ch: 不理他了。
t_en: Ignore him.

t_ch: 年轻人，回头是岸——这是我唯一能对你说的了。
t_en: Youngster, there's always a pathway back... That's all I can say to you.

t_ch: 男人突然不再聒噪，仿佛一台播音器被突然拔掉了电源；他的表情和肢体也陷入僵直。
t_en: The man suddenly clams up, like a pair of headphones that just had its cable ripped out. His expression and body both stiffens up.

t_ch: …奇怪，我感觉…突然冷静了下来。
t_en: ...Strange, I feel... suddenly very calm.

t_ch: …仔细想想，取乐有什么不好的呢？追求乐趣本来就该是生命的一部分才对。
t_en: ...Thinking carefully about it — what's wrong with having fun? The pursuit of leisure SHOULD be a part of our lives.

t_ch: 你总算正常一些了。
t_en: You're finally starting to sound normal.

t_ch: 的确，之前我一看到别人开心的样子就不冷静，好像浑身上下有虚卒在爬——这是不正常的。
t_en: Indeed, I used to lose my cool whenever I saw someone looking happy, as though voidrangers were crawling all over me — and that is not normal behavior.

t_ch: 现在你想通了？
t_en: Have you got everything figured out now?

t_ch: 不，还不能这么说……
t_en: No, I can't quite say that...

t_ch: 但必须承认，之前我一看到别人开心的样子就不冷静，好像浑身上下有虚卒在爬——这是不正常的。
t_en: But, I have to admit I used to lose my cool whenever I saw someone looking happy, as though voidrangers were crawling all over me — and that is not normal behavior.

t_ch: 真香，是吧。
t_en: Ironic.

t_ch: …那是什么意思？
t_en: ...What is that supposed to mean?

t_ch: 不过必须承认，之前我一看到别人开心的样子就不冷静，好像浑身上下有虚卒在爬——这是不正常的。
t_en: But I do have to admit I used to lose my cool whenever I saw someone looking happy, as though voidrangers were crawling all over me — and that is not normal behavior.

t_ch: 我想，我的愤怒…大概只是出于妒忌而已。
t_en: I think that this rage of mine... probably stems from envy.

t_ch: 我的家乡是一个被公司并入宇宙市场，却始终不怎么发达的星球。自出生起，我就活在巨大的落差之中……
t_en: My home planet was brought into the universal market by IPC, but was never really very developed. Ever since I was born, I've grown up among a great disparity of wealth...

t_ch: 星球本地的工作除了采矿就是种田，根本没什么高级就业岗位。所以年轻人都渴望着走出去，到广阔宇宙里寻找机遇。
t_en: The only local work available on-planet is mining or farming, with no hope whatsoever of any career advancement. So the youth all hunger to break out and search for opportunity in this vast universe.

t_ch: 可离开的路无非就那么几条：要么成为公司员工，要么加入博识学会——后者的待遇比前者更高，竞争自然也激烈许多。
t_en: But the pathways out are few and far between: Either become an IPC worker or join the Intelligentsia Guild. As the latter's salary is higher, competition is naturally more intense.

t_ch: 所以你成为了公司员工？
t_en: And so you became an IPC worker?

t_ch: 呵，怎么可能？公司员工虽然能穿越星海，待遇却跟留在母星上没什么两样。那些怀着一腔热血的年轻人，最终达成愿望的少之又少。
t_en: Er, no? IPC workers might have access to space travel but the pay is hardly better than staying on-planet. Very few of those youths brimming with enthusiasm ever get to achieve their dreams in the end.

t_ch: 所以你加入了博识学会？
t_en: And so you joined the Intelligentsia Guild?

t_ch: 没错，因此我必须为十几轮测试和答辩做准备。这远比想象中困难许多，几乎耗尽了我全部的精力。
t_en: Correct. I had to pass a dozen rounds of testing and an oral defense. It was far more difficult than I anticipated, and almost completely exhausted me.

t_ch: 躺平多好啊。
t_en: Sounds better to just quit.

t_ch: 哈，有些星球或许存在这个选择，但我的母星不包括在内——在我们那儿，不干活，就没饭吃。
t_en: Ha, that might be an option on some planets, but my home planet isn't one of them — where I'm from, if you don't work, then you don't eat.

t_ch: 当时我还年轻，也有自己喜欢的东西，比如从奥布瓦尔传入的夸克棋，比如无名客带来的开拓牌，再比如星际互联网络的电子游戏…数不胜数。
t_en: Back then I was still young, and had my hobbies such as Auberval's quark chess, trailblaze cards from the Nameless, and video games from the Interastral Network... So, so many games.

t_ch: 但我知道，如果我真接触并沉迷这些东西，就是因一时的快乐葬送了未来。
t_en: But I knew that if I ever got addicted to those things, I'd be consigning my future to ruin for the sake of temporary pleasure.

t_ch: 为了进入博识学会，我死死把控着玩乐的欲望，用全部脑力来学习、记忆…真是一段充实又压抑的时光。
t_en: To join the Intelligentsia Guild, I had to rein in tightly any desire to have fun, and devote all of my brain to study and rote learning... It was an enriching time, but also a stifling time.

t_ch: 我原以为只要成功当上了学者，对娱乐的强烈需求自然就会消失…现在看来，堵不如疏啊。
t_en: I thought that as long as I became an academic, this powerful desire of mine for entertainment would naturally subside... But now it looks like it's better to clear it away rather than bottle it all up.

t_ch: 冷静下来之后，我感觉自己体内的某个结解开了。但不知为什么，我似乎还是对一切提不起兴趣……
t_en: After I calmed down, I felt as though this almighty knot was untying inside me. And I'm not sure why, but I can't seem to arouse any interest in anything...

t_ch: 你就是压抑太久了。
t_en: You bottled everything up for too long.

t_ch: 每天玩8小时游戏就好了。
t_en: Just game for eight hours a day and you'll be fine.

t_ch: 的确， 原本我来匹诺康尼也是为了放松。但我对游戏…根本就一窍不通啊。
t_en: Indeed, I first came to Penacony to relax. But when it comes to gaming... I'm completely clueless.

t_ch: 你瞧，我积攒了这么多艾迪恩代币，却压根没有用的机会……
t_en: Just look — I've saved up this many Aideen Tokens but haven't had any chance whatsoever to use them...

t_ch: 我们一起玩游戏吧。
t_en: Let's play together.

t_ch: 什么？！我是说，真的可以吗？
t_en: What!? I mean, are you sure?

t_ch: 毕竟，我打小就没接触过这些东西，作为玩伴来说肯定不够格吧……
t_en: After all, I haven't touched those things since I was little. I'm not sure if I'm good enough to be your playmate...

t_ch: 不过既然你都这样说了，我就尽力试试看。
t_en: But if you say so, then I'll give it my best.

t_ch: 哦对，也分你一些代币吧…光凭我自己的话，想要用光它们还是太难了。
t_en: Oh yes, here are some of those tokens for you... It'd be too hard for me to use them all by myself.

t_ch: 哟，是你，我的朋友！瞅瞅，我这新形象如何？
t_en: Oh, it's you. My friend! Check it out. What do you think about my new look?

t_ch: 挺酷的。
t_en: It's pretty cool.

t_ch: 哈，那当然！这可是我问了好几个朋友以后才凑出来的搭配。
t_en: Ha, of course it is! I only put this outfit together after asking several friends about it first.

t_ch: 有点土。
t_en: It's pretty basic.

t_ch: 真、真的吗？我觉得还好啊，几个懂搭配的朋友也都觉得不错……
t_en: R—Really? I think it's fine, and a few fashionable friends all thought it was fine, too...

t_ch: 你这…变化挺大。
t_en: It's a... big change.

t_ch: 是吧？我特意请教了几个懂搭配的朋友。
t_en: Really? I made a point to ask a few friends who understand fashion.

t_ch: 既然我已经下定决心玩转匹诺康尼，穿着肯定也要符合这里的风格才行。
t_en: Seeing as I've decided to stay here in Penacony, my style has to be in line with those here.

t_ch: 之后有机会的话，你说不定能看见我的更多行头呢~
t_en: If you get the chance later on, you might just get to see a few more of my fits~

t_ch: 在梦里就该开心一点——我也该学着享受生活了。
t_en: It's a dream, so why not be happy — I should learn how to enjoy life a little more.

t_ch: 嘶…刚才可真是失态，没想到我也有喜怒无常的一天。
t_en: Tsk... I was really out of character just then. I'm not usually moody like this at all.

t_ch: 这倒是让我想起了很早以前遇见的一位艺术家，他也是这样时哭时笑、行为奇特，还时不时把周边人当作捉弄的对象……
t_en: This reminds me though of an artist who I met a long time ago. He also cried and laughed a lot, behaving strangely and sometimes poking fun of the people around him...

t_ch: 想必是很幽默的家伙。
t_en: Sounds like a really funny guy.

t_ch: 是啊，直到现在我也记忆犹新。
t_en: He sure was. I can still remember him vividly to this day.

t_ch: 是不是还戴着面具？
t_en: Does he still wear a mask?

t_ch: 对！戴着面具，做工很精致的面具。
t_en: Yes! He wears a mask, one with exquisite craftsmanship.

t_ch: 他来到我游学的星球，用表演和趣闻引发了全队的欢笑——说实话，我从来没笑得那么夸张过。
t_en: He came to the planet where I was matriculating and made the whole team laugh with his skits and anecdotes — to be honest, I had never laughed so hard before.

t_ch: 当时我觉得这种活法真不错，潇洒、快乐…于是我找到他，恳求他能带我一同旅行。
t_en: Back then I thought this kind of lifestyle was really cool, carefree, and happy... So I found him and begged him to take me traveling with him.

t_ch: 但我却吃了个闭门羹…他说我太过无趣，脑海已经被无用的知识占据，一张口就会把笑话冲淡。
t_en: But I got the cold shoulder... He said I was too boring. My mind was filled with useless trivia and I'd ruined jokes all the time.

t_ch: 或许那次经历激发了我的叛逆心，让我一听见笑声就浑身难受…谁知道呢？
t_en: Maybe that experience is what inspired my rebellious spirit, and what left me feeling uncomfortable whenever I heard others laugh... Who knows?

t_ch: 后来我在博识学会查阅了一些资料，才知道这种人被称作「假面愚者」…也许他们看见匹诺康尼这般景象，会创造出不一样的笑话？
t_en: Later on, I pulled some data at the Intelligentsia Guild only to discover that this kind of person is called a "Masked Fool"... Maybe they'll come up with some different jokes if they ever see Penacony.

t_ch: 救救孩子们！救救宇宙的未来吧！
t_en: Save the children! Save the future of the universe!

t_ch: 唉，游戏…游戏的乐趣总有尽头。到最后，宇宙只剩一片灰暗……
t_en: *sigh* Gaming... The fun of gaming must always come to an end. In the end, only darkness remains in the universe...

t_ch: 人们总是试图用快乐填满生活，好让自己活得不那么难受，我也是其中之一。我原以为游戏能帮助我重拾快乐……
t_en: People always try to fill their lives with pleasure so they can live life more comfortably, and I am one of them. At first, I thought gaming could maybe help me regain my sense of joy...

t_ch: <size=28>但什么用都没有！</size>
t_en: <size=28>But it's no use at all!</size>

t_ch: 我已经失去了感受快乐的能力——只要失去过一次，它就永远回不来了！
t_en: I've lost the capacity to feel joy — I lost it once, and it's never going to come back!

t_ch: 在如此悲惨的宇宙中，谁也不能摆脱失去快乐的必然结局，因为谁都会有无可奈何的时候！财富和假期总是不够多，麻烦和账单总是不够少。
t_en: In a universe as tragic as this, nobody gets to escape the inevitable conclusion of happiness lost — because everyone has always felt that helpless feeling before! There's never too much when it comes to riches and vacation, and never too few when it comes to troubles and bills.

t_ch: 除非…对，孩子！孩子可以无忧无虑地玩耍，只要从没被那些东西浸染过…这样的孩子还剩下多少？
t_en: Unless... Yes, children! Children get to play without a care in the world, so long as they're never infected by those things... How many such children are there left out there?

t_ch: 救救孩子们，救救宇宙的未来吧！
t_en: Save the children, and save the future of the universe!

t_ch: 要不你去加入悲悼伶人吧…
t_en: Have you ever considered signing up with the Mourning Actors...?

t_ch: 或许是个主意，也不知道他们会不会收留我这样的废物……
t_en: That's an idea, but I don't know if they'd ever want to take in anyone as useless me...

t_ch: 乐观一点啦…
t_en: Cheer up a little...

t_ch: 乐观？在快乐重新占领宇宙之前，恐怕没有人能乐观起来……
t_en: Cheer up? I'm afraid nobody will ever be able to cheer up until happiness recaptures the universe...

t_ch: 干脆永远别醒来算了…永远都别醒来……
t_en: I'd be better off never waking up at all... Never waking at all...

t_ch: 呜呼！这里的生活果然像糖浆一样甜美！
t_en: *Sob* Life here really is sweeter than syrup!

t_ch: 没有该死的考核，没有逼得人掉头发的论文，也没有整天用鼻子看人的上司……
t_en: No cursed examinations, no more pulling my hair out writing theses, no more arrogant higher-ups looking down their noses all day long...

t_ch: 美梦，这就是货真价实的美梦！我过去几十年人生里没有一次做出过这样的美梦！
t_en: Sweet dreams — well, this really is the sweetest of dreams! I haven't dreamed anything like this for dozens of years!

t_ch: 据说最近新出了一款彩虹芥末味的苏乐达，之后买一瓶尝尝看…哦，听说飞碟堡的味道也不错，得去尝尝。
t_en: I heard SoulGlad is putting out a new Rainbow Mustard flavor. I'll have to buy one and give it a try... Oh, I heard the Floatdisc Burger flavor is great too. Gotta try that.

t_ch: 啊…要体验的东西太多了！干脆永远别醒来算了…永远都别醒来……
t_en: Ah... There's just too much to experience! I'd be better off never waking up at all... Never waking at all...

t_ch: 拿出来啊！把更好玩的东西给我拿出来！
t_en: Give it to me! Give me something more fun!

t_ch: 为什么？为什么不出货？！
t_en: Why? Why not!?

t_ch: 我抽了整整四十发，没有一次出现过像样的奖励——没、有、一、次！
t_en: I pulled forty times all up, and not once did a decent prize appear — Not. One. Single. Time.

t_ch: 一定有什么地方出错了——对，是游戏，一定是游戏本身的问题！
t_en: Something must have gone wrong — yeah, the game. Something must be wrong with the game itself!

t_ch: 不能给人带来快乐的游戏，存在有什么意义？设计这些游艺项目的家伙脑子里都是浆糊吗？
t_en: What point is there in a game that doesn't bring joy to its players? Do the designers of these entertainment titles have jam for brains?

t_ch: 为什么没有我最喜欢的歼星舰大战？为什么不把更有趣的玩法拿出来？你们在害怕什么？！
t_en: Why isn't my favorite, Starnought Wars, here? Why not make the gameplay more fun? What are you cowards afraid of!?

t_ch: 哦，我懂了——是我充的钱还不够？拿去，都拿去吧！都是身外之物，我才不需要这些钞票！
t_en: Oh, I understand... I didn't top up enough credit? Then take it! Take it all! It's all just mere worldly possessions. I don't need any of this cash!

t_ch: 愚致思把口袋里的钞票扔向空中——他大概只有在梦里才能如此尽兴地挥霍。
t_en: Poseman throws all the bills from his pockets into the air — no doubt he only squanders money so crazily when he's in a dream.

t_ch: 为什么？为什么不出货？！
t_en: Why? Why not!?

t_ch: 我抽了整整四十发，没有一次出现过像样的奖励——没、有、一、次！
t_en: I pulled forty times all up, and not once did a decent prize appear — Not. One. Single. Time.

t_ch: 一定有什么地方出错了——对，是游戏，一定是游戏本身的问题！
t_en: Something must have gone wrong — yeah, the game. Something must be wrong with the game itself!

t_ch: 不能给人带来快乐的游戏，存在有什么意义？设计这些游艺项目的家伙脑子里都是浆糊吗？
t_en: What point is there in a game that doesn't bring joy to its players? Do the designers of these entertainment titles have jam for brains?

t_ch: 我小时候做的习题都比这好玩一千倍！
t_en: The schoolwork I did as a kid was a thousand times more fun than this!

t_ch: 你的假期怎么了？
t_en: How's your vacation going?

t_ch: 哦，我在学会那边还有工作要做。为了来匹诺康尼这趟，我把攒了好久的年假全请完了——也就二十多天吧。
t_en: Oh, I've still got some work to do over at the guild. I put all the annual leave that I'd been saving up into this trip to Penacony — just twenty days or so.

t_ch: 原本以为用这段时间游遍梦境绰绰有余，结果玩起来才发现…真是时光似箭，日月如梭啊。
t_en: I thought it'd be plenty of time in which to see the dreamscape, but after playing here for a while I've realized... time really is like an arrow — oh, how fast it flies.

t_ch: 不过，假如我还是像之前那样满腹怨气的话，估计只会度过一段扫兴的时光吧……
t_en: However, if I were still as sulky as before, I'm guessing that I'd probably have had a disappointing time...

t_ch: 玩得开心。
t_en: Have fun.

t_ch: 嗯，也祝你玩得开心。
t_en: Mmm, you have fun too.

t_ch: 只剩这么几天了…得赶紧抓住假期的尾巴才行。
t_en: Only a few days left... Gotta really seize the tail end of this vacation.

t_ch: 别、别过来，这里很危险！
t_en: S—Stay away. It's dangerous!

t_ch: 快去找家族的警卫！
t_en: Quick, go and fetch The Family guard!

t_ch: 呼——得、得救了…我这小身板，差点就塞了怪物的牙缝了，哈哈！
t_en: Whew... S—Safe and sound... I'm so small that I almost got stuck between its fangs, haha!

t_ch: 明知道不过是在做梦，但被那群家伙盯上还是挺刺激的…您说是吧？
t_en: Even though I know it's just a dream, it's still thrilling to have those things staring at me... Don't you think so?

t_ch: 你怎么被困在这里？
t_en: How'd you get stuck here?

t_ch: 这个嘛…说来惭愧，我近来身体抱恙，一时睡得太沉，结果睁开眼……
t_en: Oh, that... I'm ashamed to say that I'd been sick lately and I fell into too deep a sleep. So when I opened my eyes...

t_ch: 我感觉酒店的氛围似乎有些奇怪，本打算先去大堂那边看看情况，可惜还没到半路就被这群家伙围住了。
t_en: The atmosphere of the hotel felt a little bit strange to me. I'd been meaning to go to the lobby and check out everything there first, but unfortunately these things surrounded me before I could get halfway.

t_ch: 多亏您及时赶到，才让我免去一场无妄之灾。还请允许我向您致以最诚挚的感谢。
t_en: Thanks to your timely arrival, I've been spared from unexpected calamity. Please allow me to express my sincerest of gratitude to you.

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 感谢您的体贴，我亲爱的朋友。虽然很想说一切安好，但…不怕您笑话，我现在多少还是有些腿软的。
t_en: Thanks for your concern, my dear friend. Even though I'd love to say everything's fine, it's just... you can laugh, but I'm feeling a little weak in the legs.

t_ch: 即便深知这不过是一场梦，人在遭遇突发事件时，本能的反应也往往要快过理性。凑巧，我不是个胆大的人……
t_en: Despite knowing full well that it's a dreamscape, people's instinctual reactions are quicker than logical thought when it comes to emergencies. And as luck would have it, I'm not a brave person...

t_ch: 你的脸色有些差。
t_en: Your complexion looks a little off.

t_ch: 抱歉…被您看见这副狼狈的模样着实有失体面。
t_en: Sorry... It's really awkward for me to be caught showing you this side of me.

t_ch: 不瞒您说，除去方才受到的惊吓，我近来还时常被某种难以言说的疲惫感困扰……
t_en: Honestly — apart from the shock I just got — I've been having this indescribable feeling of fatigue lately...

t_ch: 你生病了？
t_en: Are you sick?

t_ch: 生病？嗯…大概是吧——如果精神不稳定能被称之为「病」的话……
t_en: Sick? Hmm... Probably — if mental health falls under the category of being "sick"...

t_ch: 遇到麻烦了？
t_en: Are you having trouble with something?

t_ch: 嗯…姑且算是吧，我也不知道该从何说起……
t_en: Hmm... Let's just say that I have no idea where to begin...

t_ch: 这里——我的脑子、我的记忆…似乎出了问题。我好像忘记了什么，但又不是那么确定。
t_en: Here... my brain and my memory... something seems to be wrong with them. I seem to have forgotten something, but then again I'm not entirely sure.

t_ch: 我最近总觉得心里空荡荡的，就像…就像胸口被凭空挖走了一块肉似的。
t_en: I just feel this emptiness in my chest all the time these days, like... just like someone's ripped this chunk of flesh out of thin air from my chest.

t_ch: 我觉得自己好像弄丢了什么东西…我本该非常讨厌它才对，可现在却只觉得怅然若失……
t_en: I feel like I've lost something... I should really hate it, but now I feel frustrated and lost...

t_ch: 啊，很抱歉自顾自说了这么多，希望没有教您感到厌烦。
t_en: Ah, I'm really sorry for blabbing on so much. Hope I didn't bore you.

t_ch: 你知道酒店怎么了吗？
t_en: Do you know what's up with the hotel?

t_ch: 这个嘛…酒店的空间似乎变得异常混乱，还出现了不少看起来不那么友好的…生物？
t_en: Oh, that... The hotel space seems to be unusually chaotic, and there are a few not very friendly-looking... creatures?

t_ch: 但说实话我倒不觉得有多奇怪，毕竟这可是梦境。又或者说，这才应该是一个梦应有的样子——混沌、奇异、不可捉摸。
t_en: But to be honest, I don't think it's strange. After all, this is a dreamscape. Or to put it another way, this is how a dream should be — chaotic, bizarre, and unfathomable.

t_ch: 当然，这仅是些个人拙见罢了，很抱歉没能为您提供更多有用的信息。
t_en: Of course, this is just my humble opinion. Sorry, I can't provide you with any more useful information.

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 虽然与您的交谈令人愉悦，但您说得对，我们还是先离开这里比较好。
t_en: While it's been a real pleasure chatting with you, what you said is right — we should get out of here soon if we know what's good.

t_ch: 我们走吧？
t_en: Are we leaving?

t_ch: 唔…虽然很抱歉，但我可能暂时还没恢复过来……
t_en: Hmm... I'm really sorry about this, but I don't think I've recovered just yet...

t_ch: 虽说此地不宜久留，但抱歉…请容我先缓缓。
t_en: Even though it's not good to stay here for long, I'm sorry but... please allow me to take my own time.

t_ch: 皮皮西原本柔和的神色于一瞬间变得尖锐，眼角蓦地燃起一抹薄红。
t_en: The Pepeshi's once-gentle expression immediately grows intense, and a dim red line suddenly lights up his eyes.

t_ch: 不知为何，你觉得在他这副皮囊之下浮现出了一个迥然不同的灵魂。
t_en: You don't know why, but somehow it feels as though a completely different soul has emerged beneath its skin.

t_ch: 哈…不过是几只小垃圾，就被吓成这副怂样，果然是个没了我就不行的废物……
t_en: Ha... It's just a few pieces of trash, but enough to get scared stiff. So it turns out they really are useless without me around...

t_ch: 怎么，平时嫌弃我嫌弃得要死，现在才几天不见就跟丢了魂一样。
t_en: What's up? You usually shun me badly, but now after just a few days of not seeing each other, you seem distracted.

t_ch: 哼，当初我说什么来着——让你小心谨慎、让你不要和人聊了几回就迫不及待地倒贴上去，现在好了吧？
t_en: Hmph, what did I say at the start — be careful, don't be clingy after chatting with someone a few times. Are you okay now?

t_ch: 虽然不知道他们究竟用了什么手段，但肯定和那女…哦，差点忘了你。
t_en: I don't know what methods they used, but they must have involved that girl... Oh, I nearly forgot about you.

t_ch: 皮皮西突然转头，落在你脸上的目光仿佛属于某种掠食性动物。
t_en: The Pepeshi suddenly turns around and the light that fell upon your face felt like it belonged to some kind of apex predator.

t_ch: 这么说来，我还得多谢你把我叫醒——你可以叫我W。
t_en: Speaking of which, I have you to thank for waking me up — you can call me W.

t_ch: 倒过来的M？
t_en: An upside-down M?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，挺敏锐的嘛！见过「我」的人都觉得我们是疯子…我是搞不懂，这有什么好大惊小怪的？每次看到他们惊慌失措的白痴样子，我都忍不住想笑！
t_en: Haha, very perceptive! Everyone who's met "me" thinks that we're nuts... I just don't get what all the fuss is about though? Every time I see those panicked idiots, I lose it and start laughing!

t_ch: 你的眼神倒是很…平静，我喜欢！我W就喜欢脑子利索，办事靠谱的家伙！所以…要不干脆好人当到底，再顺便帮我个忙吧？
t_en: Your eyes are very... peaceful. I like that! I like quick thinkers and reliable people! So... why don't you be a good person, and do me a solid?

t_ch: 在黄金的时刻有个叫阿然的梦境鼓励师，她对我来说…非常重要。
t_en: In Golden Hour, there's a Dreamscape Motivator called Ran. She is... very important to me.

t_ch: 她往常一般都在钟表小子广场附近转悠。换做平时我肯定亲自去找她，但是现在情况稍微有点…呃…*微妙*。
t_en: She's usually hanging out near Clockie Plaza. If things were normal, I'd definitely go over myself, but as it stands, things are a little... "delicate" right now.

t_ch: 怎么说，能帮哥们一把吗？我在<color=#f29e38ff>艾迪恩公园</color>等你好消息，你回来之后咱们可以一起喝杯好的！
t_en: How about it? Can you do me a solid? I'll be at the <color=#f29e38ff>Aideen Park</color> waiting for the good news. And once you're back, we can grab a drink together!

t_ch: 怎么说，你见到阿然了吗？她平时一般都在钟表小子广场附近转悠。
t_en: How's it going? Did you see Ran yet? She's usually hanging out near Clockie Plaza.

t_ch: 有个叫姆利斯的人在找你…
t_en: Someone called Mullich is looking for you...

t_ch: 等等，谁…你说谁在找我？！
t_en: Hang on, who... Who did you say was looking for me!?

t_ch: 眼前这位女士闻言瞳孔骤缩、脸色惨白，彻底失去了原本的优雅从容。
t_en: When the woman heard this name, her pupils suddenly shrank and the blood drained from her face as she lost all previous sense of grace and bearing.

t_ch: 你、你跟那家伙是一伙的？别过来，不然我要叫人了！
t_en: A—Are you in cahoots with that guy? Stay away, or I'll call out for someone!

t_ch: 你…你们应该知道，那种事如果再次发生，家族绝对不会坐视不管！
t_en: You... you oughta know that if that kind of thing ever happens again, The Family won't just sit by idly and watch!

t_ch: 哪种事？
t_en: What kind of thing?

t_ch: 不要装傻，你会不知道我在说什…等等，你…你真没骗我？
t_en: Don't play dumb. As if you don't know what I'm talking abo... Hang on, do you... Do you really not know?

t_ch: 阿然女士反复扫视着你脸上每一寸表情，半晌才如释重负般长舒了口气。
t_en: Miss Ran scanned your every expression again and again before letting out a sigh of relief after what seemed like an age — and what seemed like a heavy burden to bear.

t_ch: 呼——吓死我了…我还以为他又多了个帮手呢，本来就已经够吓人的了……
t_en: Whew — you freaked me out... I thought he'd roped in yet another accomplice. Things were already getting scary enough...

t_ch: 所以可以请您告诉我您究竟是为了什么而来的吗？
t_en: So can you please tell me what exactly it is you're doing here?

t_ch: 你将遇到姆利斯和*W*的事一五一十告知了阿然……
t_en: You spill the beans about Mullich and "W," and tell Ran the whole story...

t_ch: 「她对我来说非常重要」——他想说的怕不是「非常重要的报复对象」吧？
t_en: "She's very important to me" — I'm afraid what he meant to say was, "Taking vengeance against her is very important to me."

t_ch: 您听我说，那个人虽然看起来温和无害，但实际上是个极端的偏执狂！至于那个所谓的W，我还没真正见过他，但……
t_en: Listen to me: That guy may sound friendly and harmless, but he's actually an extremist bigot! And as for that W, I've never really met him but...

t_ch: 总之，为了您的安全，请千万不要再跟他有接触了！
t_en: In a nutshell, for your own safety, please don't have any contact with him again!

t_ch: 有这么可怕吗？
t_en: Is he that bad?

t_ch: …那是因为您还没有和他深入接触过！我认识很多精神异常者，他们中大部分都是好人，只是在不断遭受病痛的折磨…但是那家伙，完全不一样！
t_en: ...That's just because you haven't come into close contact with him yet! I know plenty of people with mental health issues and most of them are great people — they're just suffering from torturous pain... but this guy, he's completely different!

t_ch: 他就是…他就是个浓缩身高的禽兽！我恳请您不要再和他来往了……
t_en: He's just... He's just a shrunken little beast! I'm begging you, please don't have any further contact with him...

t_ch: 那你怎么办？
t_en: What about you, then?

t_ch: 我？我能怎么办…我之前已经向家族申请过庇护了，也因此过了几天安稳日子。
t_en: Me? I'm doing what I can... I applied to be put under protection before with The Family, so at least I've had a few days of relative calmness.

t_ch: 家族在处理这类问题时一向十分周到，可偏偏这次就…怪不得，怪不得那家伙会躲到白日梦酒店里去！他是害怕被猎犬家系的人给揪出来吧？
t_en: The Family is always very considerate when handling this kind of thing, but unfortunately this time... No wonder that guy went and hid in The Reverie! He's afraid of getting busted by the Bloodhound Family!

t_ch: 请、请您一定要对我的状况守口如瓶！我不想再和那家伙扯上任何关系了！
t_en: Please, you absolutely have to keep this under wraps! I don't want to ever have anything do with that guy again!

t_ch: 我、我也必须采取行动…得赶快再申请一次梦境人身保护才行……
t_en: I—I have to take my own precautions here... and apply for another round of personal dreamscape protection...

t_ch: 那些守卫是…？
t_en: Are those guards...?

t_ch: 呀？！呼…是、是您啊，吓我一跳。抱歉，我现在稍微有点…紧张。
t_en: Ah!? Whew... i—it's you. You freaked me out there. I'm sorry. It's just that I'm a little... anxious right now.

t_ch: 或许您会觉得我过于小题大做了，但那个姆利斯…唉，实话告诉您吧，他之前跟踪过我的其他客人，还对他们进行人身威胁……
t_en: You might think I'm making a mountain out of a molehill, but that guy Mullich... Well, to be honest, he stalked one of my clients before, and physically threatened them...

t_ch: 这也是为什么我不希望您和他接触…不过放心吧，家族这次又加派了人手，我们绝不会再给他伤害别人的机会。
t_en: This is why I don't want you to have any further contact with him... But don't worry. The Family has sent additional security. There's no way we're giving him any more chances to hurt someone.

t_ch: 盯紧点…
t_en: Keep on the lookout...

t_ch: 你那边有动静吗？
t_en: Any signs of activity over there?

t_ch: 那个闹事的，之前不是早就处理过了吗？这回怎么又……
t_en: Didn't that troublemaker get taken care of? Again...

t_ch: 我也觉得奇怪…该不会有什么人在暗中捣鬼吧？
t_en: Something's going on... Could it be there's someone else behind the scenes causing trouble?

t_ch: 光他一个就够让人头疼了。听说之前有个受害者从他那收到一整箱活虫子，大大小小几十种、一共上百只。那个倒霉蛋，打开箱子后直接就厥过去了。
t_en: He's enough of a handful himself. I heard one victim got an entire crate of live bugs from him — dozens of different species big and small, hundreds of them. That poor soul opened up the crate and just fainted on the spot.

t_ch: 真够缺德的。不说闲话了，接着巡逻吧。不能再让他祸害别人了。
t_en: It's just wicked. Okay, enough chit-chat. Keep up with patrols. We cannot let him harm others.

t_ch: 呦——总算来了！怎么样，有消息了吗？
t_en: Yo — finally made it back! What's up, any news?

t_ch: 我没找到她。
t_en: I couldn't find her.

t_ch: W的目光在你脸上来回逡巡，寻找欺骗的痕迹。他歪歪头，笑了起来——
t_en: W's eyes comb back and forth across your face, scouting it for signs of deception. Tilting his head, he laughs...

t_ch: 原来如此…可惜！不过没关系，我们早晚会找、到、她、的！
t_en: Is that so... What a pity! But not to worry, because we're going to find. Her. Soon!

t_ch: 她说你有病。
t_en: She said you're sick.

t_ch: 你看到W眼中有怒气划过…仿佛下一秒他就要跳起来狠敲你的脑袋了。
t_en: You see a flash of anger in W's eyes... as though he is about to jump up and knock you on the head.

t_ch: 这样啊…看来她是真的很讨厌「我们」啊。
t_en: I see... Looks like she really hates "us" then.

t_ch: 说起来，「梦境鼓励师」还真是个神奇的职业！大概也只有在匹诺康尼才能找到这个工种了吧？
t_en: Speaking of which, "Dreamscape Motivator" — what a miraculous job! I bet this line of work is only gonna be available in Penacony, right?

t_ch: 我听说，家族为了避免负面情绪污染他们「纯洁无瑕」的梦境，特聘了不少「心地善良」、「细腻敏锐」、「认真负责」的人来担任梦境鼓励师。
t_en: I heard that The Family hired a whole bunch of Dreamscape Motivators — "kind-hearted," "meticulous and perceptive," "responsible" people — to help protect that "pure and flawless" dreamscape of theirs from being contaminated by any negative mindsets.

t_ch: 至于具体怎么鼓励嘛——「鼓励师会主动发现并找到出现异常情绪波动的人群，通过沟通疗法缓解其负面情绪」——起码他们是这么宣传的。
t_en: As for how exactly they encourage people — "Motivators actively seek out people experiencing abnormal mood swings, and alleviate any negative emotions using communication therapy" — at least, that's what they say themselves.
